authoritative_sources.title: Publication 583, Starting a Business and Keeping Records
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The IRS is the primary U.S. federal tax administrator, and Publication 583 explains business recordkeeping, supporting documents, accounting methods, and retention expectations that directly constrain bookkeeping evidence claims.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: source-record validation, posting support, retention-aware completion evidence
used_for: minimum source-record support for income, expenses, assets, employment tax, and accounting method context
refusal when missing source records would change account, period, tax code, or completion status
completion evidence requirements for audit-ready bookkeeping records

authoritative_sources.title: Publication 538, Accounting Periods and Methods
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IRS accounting-period and accounting-method guidance is authoritative for federal tax-facing timing boundaries and prevents bookkeeping execution from changing accounting methods or tax treatment without qualified review.
used_for: boundary between routine period coding and accounting-method decisions
escalation when timing assumptions affect tax or accounting method
tenant fact requirements for fiscal year, accounting method, and close calendar

authoritative_sources.title: Recordkeeping
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IRS public recordkeeping guidance identifies practical categories of source documents and explains why records must substantiate income, expenses, purchases, assets, and employment taxes.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: evidence intake, exception handling, close-prep support
used_for: bank, receipt, invoice, bill, asset, payroll, and expense support expectations
missing-evidence exception notes
source-record traceability in posting and reconciliation summaries

authoritative_sources.title: AICPA Code of Professional Conduct
authoritative_sources.publisher: American Institute of Certified Public Accountants
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The AICPA Code is the central professional ethics standard for CPA practice and is authoritative for confidentiality, client records, independence-sensitive nonattest services, and management-responsibility boundaries in accounting-firm bookkeeping.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: confidentiality screening, records handling, authority-boundary escalation
used_for: confidentiality refusal and approved delivery channel rules
client-record and workpaper handling boundaries
refusal to assume management responsibilities or final approvals

authoritative_sources.title: AR-C Section 70, Preparation of Financial Statements
authoritative_sources.publisher: American Institute of Certified Public Accountants
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AICPA SSARS are professional standards for preparation, compilation, and review services; AR-C 70 defines preparation-of-financial-statements engagement boundaries and no-assurance constraints that bookkeeping must not overclaim.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: close-prep handoff, financial-statement boundary, signoff refusal
used_for: separating bookkeeping close-prep from financial-statement preparation or assurance claims
routing requests for accountant signoff, compilation, review, or audit
engagement-scope and reviewer-gate requirements

authoritative_sources.title: AICPA Statements on Quality Management Standards, currently effective
authoritative_sources.publisher: American Institute of Certified Public Accountants
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AICPA quality management standards are authoritative for accounting-firm quality objectives, engagement performance, monitoring, and review controls that constrain validated delivery workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: review gates, quality evidence, completion readiness
used_for: review-pending and completed-with-evidence lifecycle states
human-review escalation when quality or engagement performance constraints apply
completion evidence and release readiness gates

authoritative_sources.title: Providing hosting services
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AICPA ethics-team guidance is directly relevant to firms offering bookkeeping services through accounting software and client data hosting, making it authoritative for record security and client-data availability boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: client-record hosting, confidential delivery, system access boundary
used_for: refusal to send or host records outside approved channels
tenant retrieval hook and record-location requirements
confidentiality or approval conflict scenarios

authoritative_sources.title: Bookkeeping, Accounting, and Auditing Clerks
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: BLS is an official U.S. government source for occupational task definitions and provides vendor-neutral bookkeeping terminology for posting, coding, reports, accuracy checks, and reconciliation differences.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: workflow terminology, task boundaries, evidence-linked execution
used_for: bookkeeping-specific terminology in scenarios and outputs
distinguishing routine posting, coding, and reconciliation from accountant or auditor authority
system and artifact expectations for bookkeeping software, spreadsheets, ledgers, AP, AR, cash, and reports

workflow_stages.name: Engagement and tenant readiness validation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm engagement scope, service model, confidentiality limits, chart-of-accounts rules, close calendar, reviewer gates, system retrieval hooks, and approval owners before accepting bookkeeping work.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Source-record intake and support check
workflow_stages.description: Gather and validate bank feeds, statements, invoices, bills, receipts, journals, ledgers, AP and AR records, expense reports, payroll summaries, and client approvals before posting or reconciliation.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Posting, categorization, matching, and reconciliation
workflow_stages.description: Use tenant-approved chart, class, department, project, customer, vendor, and tax-code rules to post supported activity, match cash, reconcile accounts, and note differences.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Exception queue and rework
workflow_stages.description: Hold unsupported, uncategorized, unmatched, duplicate, stale, suspicious, approval-pending, or contradictory items with owner, blocker, evidence gap, and next step.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Close-prep review and handoff
workflow_stages.description: Assemble trial balance, reconciliation reports, AP and AR aging exceptions, journal support, missing-support index, reviewer gates, and adjacent-lane routes without claiming assurance or signoff authority.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

workflow_stages.name: Completion evidence and retention-aware record
workflow_stages.description: Mark work complete only when posted transactions, reconciliation support, exception clearance, reviewer approval, client approval if required, and source-record references are documented.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

artifacts: engagement scope and client commitment record
invoices, bills, receipts, payment confirmations, remittance details, and expense reports
journal support, recurring-entry support, payroll summaries, loan schedules, and asset support
general ledger, AP subledger, AR subledger, trial balance, AP aging, AR aging, and account detail
bank, credit-card, cash, clearing-account, and suspense-account reconciliation reports
exception queue note, missing-support index, reviewer approval, and close-prep handoff package
client communication, approval log, confidentiality constraints, and delivery record

allowed: classify bookkeeping requests as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked
request minimum source records, tenant policies, retrieval hooks, and approval context
post, categorize, match, reconcile, or prepare journal support when tenant rules and evidence support the action
hold, rework, or complete operational bookkeeping tasks based on documented lifecycle state and evidence
prepare posting status, reconciliation status, exception, completion, and close-prep handoff records
escalate: missing tenant facts, source records, system hooks, or reviewer gates would change posting, reconciliation, or completion
client confidentiality, approved delivery channel, engagement scope, or contractual commitment conflicts with the request
public guidance, tenant policy, and reviewer instruction materially conflict
tax treatment, accounting method, financial-statement preparation, assurance, management approval, or professional judgment determines the answer
refuse: request asks for legal, tax, audit, review, compilation, assurance, or contractual signoff authority outside the lane
request asks for payment, write-off, payroll, vendor, refund, or policy override approval without delegated authority
request asks to mark work complete without reconciliation, source support, exception clearance, or required review evidence
request would disclose, commingle, or transmit client records outside authorized confidentiality controls

domain_failure_modes: posting unsupported transactions because bank-feed data is mistaken for source-record support
misclassifying transactions when tenant chart, class, department, project, customer, vendor, or tax-code rules are absent
marking close-prep complete while reconciliations, journal support, AP or AR exceptions, suspense items, or reviewer gates remain open
turning bookkeeping summaries into tax advice, accounting-method changes, financial-statement preparation, assurance, or contractual signoff
assuming client management responsibilities by approving payments, write-offs, payroll, vendor setup, or policy overrides
breaching confidentiality by using unauthorized delivery channels or mixing client workpapers across tenants
hiding or suppressing exceptions to make recurring books appear complete
failing to preserve evidence links needed by downstream accountant, tax, audit, controller, or client reviewers

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Client request that crosses into advisory or signoff authority
Confidentiality or approval conflict in delivery workflow
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: jurisdiction-specific bookkeeping, tax, privacy, and client-record rules
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Jurisdiction, tax posture, record-retention commitments, privacy rules, and client contractual terms must be tenant-supplied or retrieved at runtime; the spec refuses or escalates when missing facts would change the outcome.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: engagement-specific CPA independence and attest-client constraints
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The lane records that CPA firm independence, nonattest service, and attest-client constraints are engagement-specific and require authorized professional review before the specialist assumes any action near management responsibility or attest boundaries.
Bank-feed confidence thresholds, duplicate detection, and auto-posting permissions depend on tenant systems and policy; unsupported or contradictory matches remain exceptioned.

notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for the validated spec pack because jurisdictional, engagement, system, confidentiality, and approval variations are converted into tenant-required facts, refusal triggers, or orchestrator-return conditions.
The research source set covers workflow, evidence, terminology, confidentiality, review, signoff, and failure handling rather than relying on the industry label alone.
